name: Tony (Tongyun) Yang
heroSpecialtyLine: I work on the hard, useful parts of AI: mostly LLM efficiency and low-data problems.

paragraphs.lead: I've spent years all over AI
paragraphs.rest: : emotion recognition, medical imaging, LLM routing, agent benchmarks. This spring I left my research fellowship on purpose. It was a hard call, but it made one thing clear: I finally know what I want to build.

: AI is shifting from a scaling race to a systems race. Bigger models still matter, but they are not enough. What counts is how we train, route, compress, and ship them. I want AI that is capable, efficient, and genuinely useful.

, I'm an independent AI researcher in Amsterdam, working with industry and academia. Before this: Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow, AI research engineer at TU Delft Imaging Physics, and an MSc from TU Delft.

intro: Open to research, in academia or industry. I care more about whether the work matters than which field it sits in.
currently: Independent AI researcher in Amsterdam, working on LLM efficiency and low-data problems.

directions.title: When data is scarce, design matters more
directions.blurb: In sensitive, low-data problems, you can't scale your way to a solution. The gains come from better architecture and smarter methods. That is the work I enjoy most: building where there isn't much to learn from yet.

directions.title: Frontier models people actually want to use
directions.blurb: Raw capability isn't the product. I work on turning frontier models into things people genuinely want to use, efficient enough to ship for real. There is a lot more worth building than another chatbot.

researchDek: These are the papers behind the two directions above. Each one took an idea and tried it for real.
projectsIntro: Here the research becomes systems people can actually run. Some are live collaborations (Gradient Networks, Tencent, MeetaVista); the rest I build on my own time. Most are still in motion.

businessCard.name: Tony Yang
businessCard.looking: Open to research · academia or industry
businessCard.tagline: Hard, low-data problems · frontier models people actually use

contactInvitation: I read every message. Write to me about research, working together, supervision, or just to say hi. I usually reply within a day, and I am easy to talk to.
